asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape 3:2 composition
Primary request: a clean editorial photograph about troubleshooting smart-home radio coverage in a real home
Scene/backdrop: warm lived-in residential living room opening into a hallway, pale plaster walls, natural oak console, linen texture, one restrained dark-green ceramic planter and a dark-green book as subtle accents
Subject: a homeowner's hand naturally plugging a compact, ordinary unbranded white smart plug into a wall outlet near the center of the frame; a small unbranded white door sensor is visibly mounted on the nearby wood doorframe; a compact unbranded local smart-home hub and home Wi-Fi router sit normally on the oak console in the middle distance; a phone rests face-up but out of focus with no readable interface
Style/medium: realistic high-end editorial residential photography, candid and practical, believable device scale and construction, natural material texture, not a studio product render
Composition/framing: wide eye-level framing, clear central focal point that remains recognizable in a landscape article-card crop, modest depth of field, uncluttered but genuinely lived in
Lighting/mood: soft natural morning window light, warm neutral color balance, calm and useful rather than futuristic
Constraints: physically plausible ordinary device types; no brand logos; no readable text; no headline or poster; no watermark; no extra hands; no device floating; no exaggerated LEDs
Avoid: decorative routing or network path lines, signal-wave overlays, charts, graphs, diagrams, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, glowing holograms, fake app screenshots, generic AI-looking smart-home diagrams, beige device lineup, surreal hardware, glossy sci-fi styling
